name: AiFi_PHY_Reconstruct
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``AiFi_PHY_Reconstruct`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: python main_BPSK.py (or main_QPSK.py / main_16QAM.py / main_64QAM.py per modulation); no documented single entrypoint or requirements.txt

examples.command: python main_BPSK.py (or main_QPSK.py / main_16QAM.py / main_64QAM.py per modulation); no documented single entrypoint or requirements.txt

dependencies: deep-learning framework (TensorFlow/Keras or PyTorch — not pinned)
algorithms: DL-based PHY-layer signal reconstruction / interference cancellation per modulation scheme (BPSK, QPSK, 16QAM, 64QAM)
MATLAB-side constellation data preprocessing (Dataprocessing.m) and BER/improvement evaluation (Improvement.m, Improvement_testing.m)
produces: Reconstructed constellation points / PHY-layer signal samples
novelty: None for WiFi recon — this is a PHY-layer signal-reconstruction / interference-cancellation research model, not a wireless reconnaissance tool. It does not sniff frames, enumerate clients, capture handshakes, or use airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy. The DL-on-commodity-PHY idea is novel in the SDR/PHY-research sense, but it is orthogonal to the KFIOSA offensive recon baseline and contributes no recon capability.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
